crime.value: Murder by throat slitting with a dirk
crime.quote: The tip of the blade made contact with his throat, breaking the skin. Blood splattered across the wall

victim.value: Sir Chase, who broke a promise to return a token to the protagonist
victim.quote: He dares to break a promise to me? I expected better from him

culprit_and_motive.value: Abigail/narrator, motivated by revenge for Sir Chase breaking his promise to return her token

resolution.value: Abigail frames herself as discovering the murder and reports it to authorities
resolution.quote: Sir, Sir, there's been a murder! Sir Chase, he lays inside his home, his throat slit open!

red_herring.value: The dirk planted at the crime scene to implicate someone else while Abigail escapes suspicion
red_herring.quote: This dirk was laying on the ground!' I held up the dirk, tears streaming down my cheeks.

clue_fairness.value: Reader knows Abigail's revenge motive and sees her acquire the dirk before the murder occurs
clue_fairness.quote: Taking revenge,' I lunged towards him, my arm swinging forward.

social_world.value: Medieval/historical class hierarchy with knights, nobility, and servants; courtship customs and social expectations
social_world.quote: Sir William has privately announced his attraction to you

alibi.value: Abigail creates false alibi by presenting herself as discoverer of the crime rather than perpetrator
alibi.quote: Sir, Sir, there's been a murder! Sir Chase, he lays inside his home, his throat slit open!

media_and_audience.value: Abigail manipulates the knight's perception by staging herself as innocent witness to the crime
media_and_audience.quote: The knight took the dirk and raced his horse to the house
